floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: lhu requirement is real but the judge's retype-to-u16 fix is CONTRADICTED by binary evidence (13+ target sites read the global with lh; se_data_set mixes lh and lhu in ONE function)

frontier.hypothesis: The value-cast respell `switch ((s16)((u16)D_800A36A4 - 2))` matches the target bytes: combine narrows the zero-extend-of-load to lhu, and the (s16) truncation emits the sll/sra 16 pair identically to the committed pun form.
frontier.mechanism: combine.c extend-of-load narrowing (zero_extend (mem:HI) -> lhu) for a single-use load.
frontier.next_probe: Swap :3331 to the value-cast form, `sandbox func_80033498 --disable all`; expect 0. This is judge-compliant (no pointer pun, no retype) and blast-radius-free.

frontier.hypothesis: The judge's header retype (s16 -> u16) is NOT viable: it flips 13+ lh read sites in other completed functions to lhu.
frontier.mechanism: type-level access width -- a u16 declaration makes every plain read an lhu.
frontier.next_probe: Already evidenced by the asm census; if desired, confirm with a single sandbox of any lh-site function (e.g. mk_leaf_newpos) after a scratch retype -- expect non-zero.

frontier.hypothesis: If the value cast fails to fold (combine declines), the committed pun is the [[pointer-alias-fake-exception]] sanctioned family needing only FAKE annotation, and se_data_set's mixed-width single-function evidence supports authenticity.
frontier.mechanism: pointer-alias-fake-exception 2026-07-01 ruling (typed re-view of a global).
frontier.next_probe: Bank the value-cast negative first, then raise the ruling_question.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Width-coercion cast `*(u16 *)&D_800A36A4` on an `s16`-declared global forces `lhu` instead of `lh` with zero semantic effect — the outer `(s16)` cast makes the expression identical to a plain read for ALL 16-bit inputs (`(s16)((u16)x-2) == (s16)(x-2)` is an arithmetic identity). This is the alias-rename injection family spelled as a use-site type-pun rather than a new declaration. Clean fix: reclassify `D_800A36A4` as `u16` in `include/code6cac.h` (consistent with other use sites: `(s8)(u16)D_800A36A4` at code6cac.c:1511 and `*(u16 *)&D_800A36A4` at code6cac_c_ab.c:333), then write `switch ((s16)(D_800A36A4 - 2))` with no cast — GCC naturally emits `lhu` for a `u16` global, reaching the target bytes in pure C.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
